commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 301 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Peugeot 301 Timing Chain Failure - Why 1.2 VTi Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 301 Access, Active 1.2 VTi (2013-2017, HMY/EB2M & HMZ/EB2F)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear combined with oil pump drive belt degradation causes chain stretch and potential valve interference.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; not suitable post-rattle

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage or post-failure units

problems.recommendation: On a Peugeot 301 worth £2,000-£4,500, a preventative chain kit makes sense if caught before rattling starts. Once noise appears or the engine has run with a stretched chain, replacement with a rebuilt unit is more reliable and cost-effective long-term.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt HMY/HMZ engine prices for your Peugeot 301

problems.h4: Peugeot 301 Oil Consumption - Why 1.6 VTi Engines Burn Oil Excessively
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 301 Active, Allure, Style 1.6 VTi (2012-2021, NFP/EC5)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring design allows oil to pass into combustion chamber; worsens with infrequent oil changes or short-trip driving.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace piston rings, recondition bores
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; underlying wear may persist

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt NFP engine with upgraded rings, fully tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution; addresses root cause comprehensively

problems.recommendation: If your 1.6 VTi is consuming over 1L per 1,000 miles and has exceeded 80k miles, investing in a rebuilt engine typically offers better long-term value than a partial ring service, especially given the 301's modest residual value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for NFP Peugeot 301 engine replacement with upgraded components

problems.h4: Peugeot 301 EGR Valve Clogging - Why 1.6 HDi Engines Lose Power
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation valve accumulates carbon deposits from diesel combustion, restricting flow and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean or replace EGR valve; reset ECU adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; may recur on high-mileage engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 9HF engine with updated EGR components, tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if engine has other wear signs or high mileage

problems.recommendation: For a 1.6 HDi under 80k miles with isolated EGR issues, cleaning or replacing the valve is cost-effective. If the engine also shows turbo wear, injector noise, or high mileage, a rebuilt unit addresses multiple failure points in one solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 9HF diesel engine prices for your Peugeot 301

problems.h4: Peugeot 301 Turbo Failure - Why 1.6 HDi Engines Lose Boost
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger oil seals degrade over time; oil starvation from delayed servicing accelerates bearing wear and shaft play.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old turbo, fit reconditioned unit, replace oil/feed lines
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block is healthy; check for secondary damage first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new turbo, upgraded seals, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if turbo failure caused oil contamination or engine damage

problems.recommendation: If turbo failure occurred suddenly with no prior warning signs and the engine was well-maintained, a standalone turbo replacement may suffice. If there was oil contamination, metallic noise, or the engine has high mileage, a rebuilt unit offers greater peace of mind.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt 1.6 HDi Peugeot 301 engines with turbo included

problems.h4: Peugeot 301 Timing Chain Tensioner - Why 1.5 BlueHDi Engines Need Attention
problems.rootCause: Early DV5RD tensioner design allows excessive chain slack; oil quality and service intervals critically impact longevity.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides with updated components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for pre-2020 DV5RD units before symptoms appear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt DV5RD with revised tensioner design, fully tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if chain has jumped or engine has run with rattling

problems.recommendation: For pre-2020 1.5 BlueHDi units, a preventative tensioner kit is excellent value if fitted before symptoms. Once rattling is audible or the engine has run with chain slack, replacement with a rebuilt unit using the revised design is the only reliable path forward.
problems.cta: Enquire about DV5RD Peugeot 301 engine replacement with updated tensioner

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 301
finalCta.paragraph: The Peugeot 301's value-focused design means engine replacement decisions hinge on cost certainty and warranty protection. EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists who supply rebuilt, reconditioned and used Peugeot 301 engines with transparent pricing and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.

Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 301 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 301 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major Peugeot 301 engine changes across its production run, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Peugeot 301 launched with two core engines: the proven 1.6 HDi diesel and the 1.6 VTi petrol, targeting budget-conscious buyers in emerging markets.
keyChanges: Peugeot 301 model introduced on PSA PF1 platform
Access and Active trim levels available at launch
knownFor: Robust 1.6 HDi block but EGR valve clogging common
1.6 VTi oil consumption issues emerging on higher mileage
Strong demand for budget replacement engines from import owners
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2013 Peugeot 301 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 301 1.6 HDi engine replacement • Peugeot 301 1.6 VTi oil consumption • 9HF EGR valve failure

years.preview: B7 Matures: 1.2 VTi Added, Trim Expansion
years.description: The 1.2L VTi petrol joined the lineup for lower-cost trims, while the 1.6 HDi remained the volume diesel choice across Access, Active and Allure variants.
keyChanges: Allure trim added with 1.6 HDi as standard
Minor updates to emissions systems for Euro 6 compliance
knownFor: 1.2 VTi timing chain stretch reports from 50k miles
1.6 HDi turbo oil seal failures on high-mileage examples
Growing replacement demand for budget-conscious import owners
years.ticker: Peugeot 301 1.2 VTi timing chain • 1.6 HDi turbo failure • NFP oil consumption fix

years.description: The facelift introduced the newer 1.5 BlueHDi diesel and 1.2 PureTech petrol engines, featuring updated emissions technology but new failure patterns on early units.
keyChanges: 1.5 BlueHDi (DV5RD) replaced 1.6 HDi in most trims
Style and VIP trims added with premium equipment
knownFor: Early DV5RD timing chain tensioner concerns pre-2020
1.2 PureTech wet belt contamination risk with incorrect oil
Higher rebuilt costs for newer engine codes but improved efficiency
years.cta: Enquire about Peugeot 301 2018-2021 facelift engine replacement
years.ticker: Peugeot 301 1.5 BlueHDi timing chain • DV5RD tensioner replacement • 1.2 PureTech wet belt service

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Peugeot 301 was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Peugeot 301 specialists.

faq.h2: Peugeot 301 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot 301 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Peugeot 301 engine typically costs £700-£2,600 supply-only, depending on engine code and condition. Supply & fit adds £400-£850 labour. Get multiple quotes to secure the best value.
keyPoints: 1.2 VTi rebuilt engines: £700-£1,700 typical range
1.6 HDi rebuilt engines: £800-£2,100 typical range
1.5 BlueHDi rebuilt engines: £1,200-£2,600 typical range
Always confirm warranty terms and component upgrades
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 301 engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Which Peugeot 301 engine is most reliable?
items.answer: The 1.6 HDi (9HF/DV6DTED) is generally the most robust Peugeot 301 engine when properly maintained, though EGR and turbo issues require attention. Regular servicing is critical for longevity.
keyPoints: 1.6 HDi benefits from widespread parts availability
Avoid short-trip driving to reduce EGR clogging risk
Use correct specification oil to protect turbo bearings
Rebuilt units with upgraded EGR/turbo components offer best reliability
items.cta: Find rebuilt Peugeot 301 1.6 HDi engines with upgraded components

items.question: What causes Peugeot 301 1.6 VTi engines to burn oil?
items.answer: Excessive oil consumption in the NFP/EC5 1.6 VTi stems from piston ring design allowing oil into the combustion chamber, worsening with infrequent oil changes or short-trip use.
keyPoints: Typical consumption: 1L per 1,000-2,000 miles on affected units
Use fully synthetic 0W-30 oil meeting PSA B71 2312 spec
Rebuilt engines with upgraded piston rings resolve the issue
Monitor oil level every 1,000 miles if consumption suspected
items.cta: Get quotes for NFP Peugeot 301 engine replacement with upgraded rings

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Peugeot 301?
items.answer: Yes, if your Peugeot 301 is otherwise in good condition and valued above £2,500. A rebuilt engine costing £800-£2,000 extends life significantly versus scrapping the vehicle.
keyPoints: Compare rebuilt engine cost against vehicle market value
Factor in remaining bodywork, interior and mechanical condition
Rebuilt engines include 12-month warranty for peace of mind
Supply & fit options simplify the replacement process
items.cta: Calculate if Peugeot 301 engine replacement makes financial sense

items.question: How do I check my Peugeot 301 engine code?
items.answer: Find your Peugeot 301 engine code on the VIN plate under the bonnet, on the engine block itself, or via DVLA lookup using your registration number on the EngineMarket quote form.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on cylinder head or block
Common codes: 9HF, NFP, HMY, DV5RD, HMZ
Enter reg on EngineMarket for instant code verification
Always confirm code matches replacement engine before purchase
items.cta: Verify your Peugeot 301 engine code instantly with reg lookup

items.question: What warranty comes with a Peugeot 301 replacement engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot 301 engines from EngineMarket suppliers include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects.
keyPoints: Warranty terms vary by supplier; confirm before ordering
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
Warranty covers internal components, not external fittings
Supply & fit labour typically covered separately by installer
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 301 engine suppliers by warranty terms

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Peugeot 301?
items.answer: Engine swaps in the Peugeot 301 are complex due to ECU, wiring and emissions compatibility. Stick to the original engine code for your variant to avoid costly conversion issues.
keyPoints: ECU programming must match engine code and emissions spec
Physical mounting points vary between engine families
DVLA notification required for major engine changes
Rebuilt original-code engines offer simplest, most reliable solution
items.cta: Find Peugeot 301 engines matching your original engine code

items.question: How long does Peugeot 301 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only Peugeot 301 engines typically dispatch within 1-3 working days. Supply & fit installations take 1-2 days at a specialist garage, depending on complexity and parts availability.
keyPoints: Confirm lead time with supplier before ordering
Allow extra time for rare engine codes or custom rebuilds
Supply & fit quotes include removal, fitting and road test
Nationwide delivery available for supply-only orders
items.cta: Get Peugeot 301 engine quotes with confirmed delivery times

items.question: Should I choose rebuilt or used for my Peugeot 301 engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot 301 engines offer better long-term value with upgraded components and warranty coverage. Used engines are cheaper upfront but carry higher risk of hidden wear.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: £700-£2,600 with 12-month warranty, upgraded parts
Used: £450-£1,400, variable mileage, shorter warranty typical
Rebuilt addresses known failure points proactively
Used suitable only for very low-budget, short-term fixes
